meta.next_version_trigger: new bsip1 schema version OR new retailer added OR 3 consecutive runs expose uncovered gap

verdict_types.hard_fail: Blocks BSIP2 entry. Must be resolved and check re-run before proceeding.
verdict_types.warning: Does not block BSIP2 entry. Must be recorded in QA report and communicated to BSIP2 operator.
verdict_types.known_issue: Expected pattern. Visible in report, generates no verdict.

domains.name: Scope Integrity
domains.description: Verifies that the canonical corpus contains only in-scope products and that the exclusion decision record exists.

checks.name: Corpus filter is locked
checks.description: corpus_filter.md exists and contains a lock marker ('Locked at:' or '*This document is locked.*')
checks.pass_condition: File exists AND contains lock marker
checks.fail_condition: File absent OR present but no lock marker
checks.automation_note: Simple file existence + text search — low priority; manual is fast

checks.name: Exclusion register exists
checks.description: candidate_review.csv exists in observations_bsip0/{retailer}/ and contains a header row
checks.pass_condition: File exists AND has header row
checks.fail_condition: File absent
checks.automation_note: File existence check — first candidate for automation alongside SCP-001

checks.name: No known out-of-scope products in approved corpus
checks.description: Products approved in candidate_review.csv (approved_for_scrape=YES) do not match hard exclusion terms from corpus_filter.md. Exclusion terms are category-specific; defaults below apply to Hummus.
checks.pass_condition: No approved product name_he matches any exclusion term
checks.fail_condition: One or more approved products match a hard exclusion term
checks.automation_note: High priority — load approved rows from CSV, check name_he against term list. Category-specific term list must be injected per run.

checks.name: Product count within expected corpus range
checks.description: Total BSIP0 product files in observations_bsip0/{retailer}/ (excluding all_discovered_raw_*.json and candidate_review.csv) is within the range defined in corpus_filter.md
checks.pass_condition: count >= hard_minimum AND count <= expected_maximum
checks.fail_condition: count < hard_minimum
checks.automation_note: File count + threshold lookup — high priority for automation

checks.name: No multi-pack or catering-size products
checks.description: No approved BSIP0 product has multi-pack marketing or catering sizes (weight_g >= 1000)
checks.pass_condition: No product has name_he containing multi-pack signals AND no product has weight_g >= 1000
checks.fail_condition: Any product matches multi-pack signals or catering weight
checks.automation_note: String match on name_he + weight_g check — straightforward

domains.name: Traceability
domains.description: Verifies that every BSIP1 enriched record can be traced back to its BSIP0 source observation.

checks.name: Every BSIP1 file has a populated source_retailers field
checks.description: In every bsip1_*.json in 03_operations/bsip1/run_{category}_001/output/, the source_retailers array is non-empty
checks.pass_condition: source_retailers is a non-empty list for all files
checks.fail_condition: Any file has source_retailers = [] or field absent
checks.automation_note: JSON field check on all output files — part of the core traceability validator

checks.name: Every BSIP1 barcode traces to a BSIP0 source file
checks.description: The barcode extracted from each bsip1_{BARCODE}.json filename corresponds to an observation file in observations_bsip0/{retailer}/. Shufersal uses P_{code}.json; Yohananof uses {barcode}/ directory.
checks.pass_condition: For every BSIP1 file, a matching BSIP0 observation exists in at least one retailer folder
checks.fail_condition: Any BSIP1 barcode has no matching BSIP0 observation across all scraped retailers
checks.automation_note: HIGHEST PRIORITY automation candidate. Cross-directory filename matching; catches silent provenance breaks.

checks.name: ingredients_raw_provenance.source is bsip0_scrape for all products with ingredients
checks.description: Where ingredients_list is non-empty in BSIP1 output, ingredients_raw_provenance.source must equal 'bsip0_scrape'
checks.pass_condition: All files with non-empty ingredients_list have provenance.source = 'bsip0_scrape'
checks.fail_condition: Any file has provenance.source != 'bsip0_scrape'
checks.warn_condition: ingredients_raw_provenance field absent when ingredients_list is non-empty
checks.automation_note: JSON field path check — part of core traceability validator

checks.name: BSIP0 source files retain scraped_at timestamp and source_url
checks.description: Every BSIP0 P_*.json (or {barcode}/discovery.json for Yohananof) has a non-null scraped_at ISO timestamp and a non-empty source_url containing the retailer domain
checks.pass_condition: All approved product files have non-null scraped_at and non-empty source_url with retailer domain
checks.fail_condition: Any approved product missing scraped_at or source_url
checks.automation_note: JSON field presence check on BSIP0 files — straightforward

checks.name: audit_ref file exists for every BSIP1 product
checks.description: Every BSIP1 product file has an audit_ref field, and the referenced bsip1_audit_{barcode}.json file exists in the same output directory
checks.pass_condition: audit_ref field present AND referenced file exists
checks.warn_condition: audit_ref present but referenced audit file absent
checks.fail_condition: audit_ref field absent entirely from any product file
checks.automation_note: Low automation priority; audit file absence is rare and easy to spot manually

domains.name: Coverage
domains.description: Verifies that the corpus has sufficient nutritional, ingredient, image, and barcode data to support BSIP2 scoring.
domains.coverage_thresholds_note: Thresholds below are Hummus defaults. Other categories define their own in corpus_filter.md.

checks.name: Nutrition coverage
checks.description: normalized_nutrition_per_100g in BSIP1 contains non-null values for all four core macros for at least 80% of products. Hard fail threshold is 60%.
checks.automation_note: Null-check on four macro fields across all BSIP1 output — straightforward

checks.name: Ingredient list coverage
checks.description: ingredients_list in BSIP1 is non-empty for at least 70% of products
checks.automation_note: Array length check across all BSIP1 output

checks.name: Image coverage
checks.description: image_url in BSIP1 is non-null and non-empty for at least 90% of products
checks.automation_note: Null/empty string check on image_url

checks.name: Barcode quality — EAN-13 vs retailer internal ID ratio
checks.description: Checks barcode_validation_status across BSIP1 output. Malformed barcodes (non-numeric, length < 4) are hard fail. >50% retailer internal IDs is a warning.
checks.automation_note: Status field check + ratio calculation

checks.name: Sodium and sugar coverage (informational)
checks.description: sodium_mg coverage >= 60% and sugars_g coverage >= 50%. Informational only — affects BSIP2 red-label detection quality.
checks.automation_note: Extends COV-001 null-check to two additional fields

domains.name: Canonical Structure
domains.description: Verifies that the canonical BSIP1 output folder is correctly populated and that excluded products have not leaked into it.

checks.name: canonical_bsip1 folder exists and is populated
checks.description: 02_products/{category}/canonical_bsip1/ exists and contains at least as many bsip1_*.json files as the BSIP0 approved product count (within 10% tolerance for BSIP1 enrichment failures)
checks.pass_condition: folder exists AND file_count >= floor(bsip0_approved_count * 0.90)
checks.fail_condition: folder absent OR file_count = 0 OR file_count < floor(bsip0_approved_count * 0.90)
checks.automation_note: Directory + file count check

checks.name: Manifest file exists and entry count matches file count
checks.description: A manifest.json or manifest.md exists in 02_products/{category}/canonical_bsip1/ and its entry count matches the actual file count
checks.pass_condition: manifest exists AND entry_count == actual_file_count
checks.fail_condition: manifest absent
checks.automation_note: Manifest generation should be part of the BSIP1 runner; validation is then a count comparison

checks.name: schema_version consistent across all canonical files
checks.description: Every bsip1_*.json in canonical_bsip1/ has schema_version equal to the approved BSIP1 schema version
checks.pass_condition: unique(schema_version values across all canonical files) == {approved_version}
checks.fail_condition: Any file has a different or absent schema_version
checks.automation_note: Single field value check across all files — trivial to automate

checks.name: No excluded products in canonical_bsip1
checks.description: Products with approved_for_scrape=NO in candidate_review.csv are not present in canonical_bsip1/
checks.pass_condition: No barcode/code from rejected rows in candidate_review.csv matches any canonical file
checks.fail_condition: Any rejected barcode/code found in canonical_bsip1 filenames
checks.automation_note: Cross-reference CSV rejected codes against canonical filenames — part of the traceability validator scope

checks.name: All canonical files have file_type 'product'
checks.description: file_type field in every canonical file equals 'product'. Audit files (bsip1_audit_*.json) must not be in the canonical folder.
checks.pass_condition: unique(file_type values) == {'product'}
checks.fail_condition: Any file has file_type != 'product'
checks.automation_note: Single field value check — trivial

domains.name: Regression Checks
domains.description: Verifies that BSIP1 output contains no fields from a later pipeline stage (BSIP2, frontend) and that no schema changes were introduced without approval.

checks.name: No BSIP2 scoring fields in BSIP1 output
checks.description: None of the BSIP2 scoring field names appear as top-level keys in any canonical BSIP1 file
checks.pass_condition: No forbidden field name appears as a top-level key in any canonical file
checks.fail_condition: Any forbidden field found
checks.automation_note: HIGHEST PRIORITY for regression automation. Grep or JSON key union check. One-line Python.

checks.name: No frontend or view-model fields in BSIP1 output
checks.description: None of the frontend/view-model field names appear as top-level keys in any canonical BSIP1 file
checks.pass_condition: No forbidden field name appears as a top-level key in any canonical file
checks.fail_condition: Any forbidden field found
checks.automation_note: Same pattern as REG-001 — can be combined into a single forbidden-field scanner

checks.name: enrichment_version matches approved BSIP1 version
checks.description: enrichment_version in all canonical BSIP1 files equals the approved version string
checks.pass_condition: unique(enrichment_version values) == {approved_version}
checks.fail_condition: Any unapproved version string found
checks.warn_condition: Version string is unrecognised (not in known version history)
checks.automation_note: Extends CAN-003 pattern — same field value consistency check

checks.name: No unexpected top-level fields in BSIP1 output
checks.description: The union of top-level keys across all canonical files does not contain any field not in the approved bsip1_v0_1 schema field list
checks.pass_condition: union(all top-level keys) is a subset of approved_top_level_fields
checks.warn_condition: Unexpected field found that is NOT in REG-001 or REG-002 forbidden lists (those would be hard fail)
checks.fail_condition: Unexpected field matches a forbidden field from REG-001 or REG-002
checks.automation_note: Key union check — Python one-liner; run after REG-001 and REG-002 pass

checks.name: BSIP0 source files are unmodified after scrape
checks.description: Filesystem modification time (mtime) of BSIP0 product JSON files does not significantly exceed the scraped_at timestamp in the file. BSIP0 files are immutable once written.
checks.pass_condition: mtime <= scraped_at + 300 seconds for all files (allowing for filesystem timestamp artefacts on copy/move)
checks.warn_condition: mtime is 5 minutes to 24 hours after scraped_at
checks.fail_condition: mtime is > 24 hours after scraped_at AND file content shows manual field edits (non-scraper format values)
checks.automation_note: Low automation priority; filesystem mtime checks are fragile across copy/backup operations. Manual spot-check is more reliable.

known_acceptable_issues.reason: Serving size is optional in bsip1_v0_1. Israeli retail labels often omit it.
English name is not required. Bari is Hebrew-primary.
Expected for short-code Shufersal products and 16000-prefix Yohananof codes. Documented in barcode_confidence_reason.
Expected on single-retailer scrape (Shufersal only). Multi-source enrichment improves on Yohananof supplementary pass.
Expected. canonical_risk_flags: ['single_source_only'] documents the reason. Does not indicate bad data.
Non-fatal enrichment observations. Reviewed in BSIP2 operator briefing.
known_acceptable_issues.action: Pass to BSIP2 operator for awareness

known_acceptable_issues.pattern: ingredient_text_quality = 'noisy' for a minority of products
known_acceptable_issues.reason: Retailer HTML sometimes embeds nutrition text in the ingredient field. BSIP1 flags but does not fail.

known_acceptable_issues.pattern: nutrition_consistency_warnings present for minor rounding differences
known_acceptable_issues.reason: Rounding artefacts from retailer display. Not a data error.

automation_priority_ranking.name: Cross-pipeline traceability validator
automation_priority_ranking.rationale: Most likely to catch silent data errors — products enriched from wrong source, provenance broken, excluded products leaked into canonical. Cross-directory filename matching. One Python script covers both checks.

automation_priority_ranking.name: Forbidden field scanner
automation_priority_ranking.rationale: Catches the most damaging class of error — BSIP2 or frontend fields leaking into BSIP1 output. Simple JSON key union check; one grep command or five lines of Python.

automation_priority_ranking.name: Coverage reporter
automation_priority_ranking.rationale: Already partially implemented in 03_audit_bsip0_hummus.py for BSIP0. Extending to BSIP1 output reuses the same pattern with different field paths.

automation_priority_ranking.name: Scope integrity scanner
automation_priority_ranking.rationale: Exclusion term matching and count thresholds. Requires category-specific term injection but the pattern is reusable. Highest operational value for Wave 2+ categories.

automation_priority_ranking.name: Canonical structure validator
automation_priority_ranking.rationale: File count, manifest consistency, schema version checks. Simple directory + JSON scans. Lower priority because canonical structure failures are usually visible during BSIP1 run completion.
